predictable_tokens.description: Predictable token/secret generation -- timestamp-based, sequential, weak PRNG, or insufficient entropy in security-sensitive tokens

probes.description: Request two tokens in rapid succession and compare -- sequential (token2 = token1 + 1) or timestamp-correlated (similar epoch prefix) indicates weak generation. Manual analysis required.

weak_session_id.description: Weak session ID generation -- short length, limited charset, predictable pattern in Set-Cookie session values

probes.description: Collect 10+ session IDs and analyze entropy -- short IDs (<32 chars), hex-only, or sequential patterns indicate weak generation. Check if session ID changes after login (session fixation prevention).

uuid_v1_leak.description: UUID v1 information leak -- UUIDv1 contains MAC address and timestamp, allowing user tracking and time correlation

probes.description: Check if UUIDs follow v1 format (time-based) -- 8-4-4-4-12 hex with version nibble = 1 (xxxxxxxx-xxxx-1xxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx). Extract MAC from last 12 chars and timestamp from first segments.

low_entropy_session_cookie.description: Set-Cookie session value is short (<24 chars) and hex-only — entropy too low for cryptographic session ID. Production sessionId is typically 32+ chars base64.

probes.description: Set-Cookie session cookie matches short-hex pattern — strong signal of insecure_randomness if session_id 16 bytes or shorter.

reset_token_short_url.description: Password-reset URL contains a token under 16 chars or hex-only ≤24 chars — likely brute-forceable.

probes.description: Reset URL with short / low-entropy token shape. Worth a brute-force calculation per program policy.

description: Insecure randomness — UUIDv1 leak / short session ID / short reset token in URL. Promoted to active W12.
